headline: How Ranch Dressing Took Over America (And Failed Everywhere Else)
summary: Ranch is Americas favorite dressing. But in the rest of the world? Its practically nonexistent. *Want more "boring" foods that can make you money? Check out our full list:* How did this creamy, herby condiment go from an obscure invention by a plumber in 1949 Alaska to a defining flavor of American consumerism? The real story behind America's obsession with Ranch is a brilliant business case. It all changed when Clorox yes, the bleach company bought a small, niche product called Hidden Valley Ranch for $8 million in 1972. Clorox took their consumer-packaged-goods playbook heavy marketing spend, massive distribution, and relentless line extensions and used it to conquer Americas salad and snack aisle. Find out how a bleach company applied the lessons they learned from P&G to turn a regional oddity into the national standard, making Ranch Americas favorite dip for wings, pizza, and everything else. headline: Don’t Look Now, But 4 Blue-Chip Giants Could Be the Newest Dividend Kings
summary: Companies that have raised dividends for shareholders for 50 years or more are the kinds of investments passive income investors need to own. Dependability is crucial for individuals seeking to increase their annual income through dividend stock investments. The Dividend Kings are the 57 companies that have raised their dividends for at least 50 years, ... Don’t Look Now, But 4 Blue-Chip Giants Could Be the Newest Dividend Kings
